pattern: Build a 2-D prefix sum table first, then each block sum is a constant-time rectangle query via inclusion-exclusion on four prefix-sum corners.
hints: Computing each cell's block sum independently would be O(k^2) per cell; a prefix sum table lets you answer each rectangle query in O(1).
Build prefix[i][j] = sum of all mat values in rows 0..i-1, cols 0..j-1. Then the sum of any rectangle (r1,c1)..(r2,c2) is prefix[r2+1][c2+1] - prefix[r1][c2+1] - prefix[r2+1][c1] + prefix[r1][c1].
For answer[i][j], clip the rectangle boundaries with max/min to stay inside the matrix, then apply the prefix-sum rectangle formula.

edge_inputs.description: k larger than both dimensions — whole matrix sums everywhere
k=0 means each cell sums only itself

stress.description: random 50x50 matrix with k up to 30
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, size)
    n = max(1, size)
    mat = [[rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 30)
    return [mat, k]
